calendar.matters:
Notice Of Seaview Insurance Companys Motion And Motion To Set Aside Summary Judgment And Vacate Bail Bond Forfeiture And Exonerate Bond Pursuant To Penal Code 1305(A)
Off Calendar Feb-05-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: REAL PARTY IN INTEREST SEAVIEW INSURANCE COMPANY's Motion To Set Aside Summary Judgment And Vacate Bail Bond Forfeiture And Exonerate Bond Pursuant To Penal Code 1305(A).

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel, or Interested Person. The court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Petitioner Seaview Insurance Company seeks an order setting aside summary judgment, vacating bail bond forfeiture, and exonerating a bond. The court grants Seaview's request for judicial notice and also takes judicial notice of Exhibit A to the People's opposition. The motion is granted, summary judgment is vacated, the forfeiture is set aside, and the bond is exonerated.

Seaview posted bond number SV25-5396691 on behalf of Javier Erazo in a criminal case. On October 31, 2024, the Erazo failed to appear in court, and the court issued a bench warrant. (RJN Ex. B.) The court did not state whether bond was ordered forfeit. (Id.) On December 16, 2025, the court ordered summary judgment and directed payment to the court on the amount of the bond.

The trial court's failure to state that bond is forfeit in open court renders it without jurisdiction to act on a forfeiture. (People v. National Auto & Casualty Insurance Co. (2002) 98 Cal.App.4th 277, 290.) The People contend Seaview can no longer seek relief because it failed to seek to set aside the forfeiture within the 180-day appearance period plus the additional tolling period. The court finds this argument unpersuasive because an act without jurisdiction is not merely voidable but void. If no forfeiture ever occurred, and National Auto holds it did not, then Seaview is not bound by the period to set aside a forfeiture. The court exonerates the bond because this was the result in National Auto. (Id., at p. 291.)

A proposed order has been submitted as indicated in the court's tentative ruling.

Judge: Christine Van Aken. Clerk: Robert Woods. Not Reported. (301/CVA)
